title: Execution of Decisions under Revised POEA Rules and Regulations
content:
SECTION 192. Issuance of Writ of Execution. - After the Order has become final and executory, the Administration shall issue a writ of execution to enforce monetary awards and/or fines imposed in the decision, either upon motion or on its own initiative.

SECTION 193. Issuance, Form and Contents of a Writ of Execution. - The writ of execution is issued in the name of the Republic of the Philippines, requiring the Enforcement Officer to execute Orders of the Administrator or authorized representative. It must contain the dispositive portion of the Order and require service upon the losing party or any person required by law before satisfaction of judgment. Execution proceeds against assets of the losing recruitment agency in this order: supersedeas bond, escrow deposit, personal property, real property. No writ is necessary for enforcement of Orders for return of travel and related documents (copy of Order served is sufficient) or where a cash bond was posted in relation to an appeal (certified copies and official receipt suffice). The writ is valid for 60 calendar days from issuance.

SECTION 194. Motion to Cancel/Quash Writ of Execution. - The judgment debtor may file a Motion to Cancel/Quash within 5 days from receipt of the writ on meritorious grounds. Filing does not stay execution unless a cash or surety bond equivalent to the judgment award/fine is posted. An Order denying such motion is final; no further similar motions entertained.

SECTION 195. Enforcement of Writs. - Enforcement Officers shall strictly follow the Manual of Instructions for Enforcement Officers of the POEA when executing Orders.

SECTION 196. Garnishment. - Enforcement Officers shall issue a notice of garnishment to persons/entities holding property of the licensed recruitment agency, directing them not to dispose of such property except to the enforcement officer. For multiple writs against the same agency, satisfaction of claims against escrow deposit is on a "first come, first serve" basis regardless of case or decision dates; simultaneous garnishments result in pro-rated distribution. Fines against cancelled/delisted agencies are subject to garnishment of escrow deposits.

SECTION 197. Return of Writ of Execution. - Enforcement Officers must submit a return immediately upon satisfaction of the judgment award, or no later than 60 calendar days from issuance. The return must state mode of service, name of person served, date of receipt, and full name of the officer who served the writ. The return forms part of case records.
